    @classmethod
    def _no_duplicate_entries(cls, value: list[str], info: ValidationInfo) -> list[str]:
        seen: set[str] = set()
        dupes: set[str] = set()
        for entry in value:
            if entry in seen:
                dupes.add(entry)
            seen.add(entry)
        if dupes:
            raise ValueError(f"{info.field_name} contains duplicate entries: {sorted(dupes)}")
        return value

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _docs_must_be_resolvable(self) -> TaskSpec:
        """If a task names doc ids, they must be resolvable — either via a
        ``docs_fixture`` or inline ``initial_state['docs']`` — so the loader
        never silently drops referenced docs."""
        if self.available_docs and self.docs_fixture is None and "docs" not in self.initial_state:
            raise ValueError(
                "available_docs is set but there is no docs_fixture and no inline "
                "initial_state['docs']; the referenced doc ids cannot be resolved"
            )
        return self

TRA-24 Build policy corpus and controlled retrieval layer

Intended owner: Evan Yang ## Context The canonical failure depends on controlled retrieval of current and deprecated refund policies. TRACE needs a small, pinned corpus with stable provenance before the runner and verifier can produce meaningful evidence. ## Objective Build the Month 1 policy corpus and controlled retrieval layer, including `search_docs`. ## Scope Include current refund policy, deprecated refund policy, resolved incident document, stable document and section IDs, status metadata, controlled ranking, and retrieval logs. ## Deliverables * Pinned policy document corpus. * Retrieval chunk schema with provenance. * `search_docs` tool implementation. * Configurable retrieval ordering for tests. * Retrieval event data needed by trace logging. ## Acceptance Criteria * Current, deprecated, and resolved documents are distinguishable. * Retrieval order can be controlled for tests. * Every returned chunk includes provenance. * Retrieved docs are traceable to the corpus. * No silent truncation. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start with deterministic keyword/BM25 or lightweight local fixtures. Do not add a hosted vector database unless the experiment requires the reviewed implementation; FAISS, Chroma, or SQLite-backed fixtures are optional only if they materially help. ## Collaborators / Reviewers Emily Au, Rupert Maiti, Karan Gupta, Skye Haik. ## Dependencies Requires initial task/document requirements from TaskSpec. ## Handoff / Blocks Blocks first task suite execution, verifier source-precedence checks, and full runner/tool integration. ## Out of Scope Hosted vector DB infrastructure and broad production RAG evaluation.

TRA-67 Define live-agent readiness criteria for Gemini and non-fixture evaluation runs

## Context TRACE's `main` branch has a deterministic fixture-based vertical slice. That is valuable, but the next product question is when a real model/provider run should count as a meaningful TRACE evaluation instead of just an API smoke test. Open Gemini work (`TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a)) is the first live-adapter step. the reviewed implementation depends on runner transcript shape, model-call timeout, provider flags, trace events, environment behavior, verifier readiness, artifact read paths, and controlled RAG/tool behavior. Without explicit readiness criteria, the team can easily merge a live adapter that technically calls a model but does not produce enough evidence for verification, attribution, failure bundles, regression, or dashboard replay. PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) (`TRA-23`) and PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) (`TRA-24`) are part of live readiness too: a Gemini run against unstable tools or uncontrolled retrieval is only an API smoke test, not a TRACE-ready evaluation. ## Objective Define the readiness criteria for live Gemini and future non-fixture evaluation runs. Realizes the `# TODO(Samrath/tracing)` in `src/trace_harness/tracing/artifact_store.py:28` — a run index file so consumers can list runs cheaply without scanning every `runs/{run_id}/` directory. **Scope (storage / write side only):** * Define a `RunIndexEntry` (Pydantic v2): `run_id`, `task_id`, `status`, verifier verdict, key timestamps, `schema_version`. * Maintain `runs/index.json` — one entry per run, written/updated atomically on run finalize via the existing `ArtifactStore.write_json` (tmp + fsync + `os.replace`, shipped in PR #54). * Reader + round-trip test; tolerate a missing/empty index (rebuildable from `runs/` directories). **Out of scope:** the read API that exposes listing — that's [TRA-20](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-20/expose-run-data-read-apis-for-dashboard-and-evaluation) (run-data read APIs), which consumes this index. ## Context The dashboard and evaluation workflows are about to depend on a stable run-read path. That path is currently split across merged code, open PRs, and follow-up Linear issues: * On `main`, `ArtifactStore.list_runs()` scans run directories and `artifact_store.py` still has a TODO for a run index. * PR #80 re-lands `run_index.py` because the previous run-index merge was orphaned and never reached `main`. * PR #79 adds a typed `RunReader` service and CLI `list-runs`, deliberately before FastAPI. * `TRA-63` plans to flip RunReader to read `index.json` and fold in verifier verdicts after PRs #79 and #80 land. * `TRA-62` is still needed so the dashboard has a frontend trace-event contract matching backend `TraceEvent` and step IDs. This is a high-risk integration surface because every dashboard view, verifier evidence reference, attribution marker, and demo run list depends on it. ## Objective Perform a deep technical audit of the run artifact read path, run-index plan, and trace-event dashboard contract so Samrath and Skye have exact implementation guidance and Justin has a clear system-level risk assessment. ## Scope Inspect and compare: * `ArtifactStore` artifact names, missing-artifact behavior, and `list_runs` behavior. * Pending `RunReader` surface and expected summary/list/fetch methods. * Pending `RunIndexEntry` / `index.json` fields and recovery behavior. * `TraceEvent` and `TraceEventType`, including `model_response` and step ID semantics. * Dashboard TypeScript contracts that already exist and the missing trace-event contract. * How verifier evidence, attribution markers, failure cards, and repair/regression previews point back to trace steps. ## Deliverables * Run-read path audit map: ```text Use case | Current code path | Pending PR/issue | Missing artifact state | Consumer | Risk | Required test ``` * Recommendation on merge/implementation sequencing for `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, and `TRA-62`. * Exact fields the frontend `trace-event.ts` contract must include to support evidence/attribution/failure-card rendering. * Missing-state policy for dashboard consumers: unknown run, partial run, verifier absent, attribution absent, bundle absent. * Concrete test suggestions for Samrath/Skye to prove run listing, run details, trace replay, and evidence step linking work. ## Acceptance Criteria * Audit distinguishes current `main` behavior from PR #79/#80 behavior and `TRA-63` follow-up behavior. * Every dashboard-facing run-data field has a named backend source or is explicitly marked unavailable. * Step ID linking is checked across trace events, verifier evidence, attribution result, and failure card evidence. * Missing-artifact states are specified in a way the dashboard can render without crashing or lying. * Samrath and Skye receive concrete next actions, not just a broad integration concern. * Justin can use the result in the systems compatibility matrix and readiness risk log. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start with: * `src/trace_harness/tracing/artifact_store.py` * `src/trace_harness/tracing/events.py` * PR #79 `src/trace_harness/run_reader.py` * PR #80 `src/trace_harness/tracing/run_index.py` * `docs/future_api.md` * `docs/future_dashboard.md` * `apps/dashboard/src/types/` * `tests/test_artifact_store.py` and pending run-reader/run-index tests in PRs Use this rule: the dashboard should read documented run artifacts through a stable interface, not invent its own file-scraping interpretation. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Justin: systems-level matrix and risk prioritization. * Samrath: ArtifactStore, RunReader, run index, read contracts. * Skye: dashboard data contracts and trace replay UX. * Rupert: runner-generated events and run_result semantics. * Karan/Darrel/Samir: evidence, attribution, and failure bundle consumers. ## Dependencies No hard blocker for the audit. Final implementation recommendations depend on whether PRs #79 and #80 land as written. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, `TRA-62`, `TRA-31`, and Justin's broader systems integration work. ## Out of Scope * Implementing FastAPI before the documented Python service/read path is stable. * Rewriting dashboard UI components. * Changing artifact layout without Samrath/Skye/Justin review.
